ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT EMMALYN S. MUNJAR'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT EMMALYN MUNJAR'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO BEING REFILED. THE MOTION IS UNTIMELY UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 473 AND DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHOWN THAT SHE IS ENTITLED TO RELIEF ON THE GROUNDS OF EQUITABLE CONSIDERATIONS. COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE PROPOSED ORDER MIRRORING THE COURT'S ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077.

calendar.matters:
Mtn For Relief From Default
Off Calendar Oct-17-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ALEXANDER PEREZ, ESQ., LAW OFFICES OF ALEXANDER PEREZ, 415.682.2540, FOR PLAINTIFF BAUMGARTNER.
KYNDRA MILLER, ESQ., MILLER ENTERTAINMENT GROUP, 877.862.0572, FOR DEFENDANT EMMALYN MUNJAR.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling with modification: DEFENDANT EMMALYN MUNJAR'S Motion For Relief From Default AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT is denied without prejudice to being refiled. The motion is untimely under Code of Civil Procedure 473 and defendant has not shown that she is entitled to relief on the grounds of equitable considerations. Counsel for the plaintiff to prepare proposed order mirroring the court's order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.

MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-11-2012 Dismissal - No Appearance

MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-10-2012 Set For Trial On Jun-11-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion Anmd Otion To Schedule Defautl Jdugemtn Oral Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Mar-13-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Court Trial/Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Judge: Donald J. Sullivan, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Sonia Wong #11288.
Alex Perez, Esq. appeared for and with the plaintiff John Baumgartner, was placed under oath and examined.
After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against the Defaulted Defendant Emmalyn S. Munjar as follows:

Damages - $500,000.00
Punitive Damages - $1,000,000.00, for a total judgment in the amount of $1,500,000.00

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
